title: laggera squarrosa
intro: Laggera squarrosa, also known as African tea, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Laggera squarrosa is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy in many African countries. The plant can be prepared for consumption as a tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or it can be used to make a decoction by boiling its roots in water.
